name: Million.js (React Performance Optimizer)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Aiden Bai; Million Lint is commercial

performance.notes: Claims 70% faster renders vs React for list-heavy components

agent_readiness.notes: Drop-in React performance optimization. block() HOC wraps React components with virtual DOM bypass. Compiler mode: million/compiler vite/webpack plugin auto-optimizes. import { block } from 'million/react'. Works with React 16+, Next.js, Vite. Optimizes components that render many similar elements (lists, tables, data grids). 70% faster rendering claim for list-heavy UIs. Million Lint (commercial) IDE plugin finds performance issues.

gotchas: block() HOC has limitations (no useState/useEffect inside)
Performance gains only significant for frequently re-rendering list components
best_when: React apps with large lists or data tables that re-render frequently
Dashboard apps with real-time data updates
Performance optimization without migrating to Solid.js/Svelte
Adding to existing React app to optimize hot paths
avoid_when: Simple apps with infrequent renders (no benefit)
When migrating to Solid.js/Svelte provides better ROI
Apps using many React hooks inside list items (block() limitations)
summary: Million.js optimizes React component rendering by bypassing the virtual DOM for block() wrapped components. 70% faster renders for list-heavy UIs. Drop-in: import { block } from 'million/react'. Compiler plugin auto-applies optimizations. Works with existing React code. No migration needed. Best for dashboards/data grids with frequent re-renders. Million Lint (commercial) finds performance issues in IDE.
